The first-deadline funnel makes my very first capture a fork-in-the-road decision (Uni? Job? Gym? Unfiled?) when all I wanted was to not forget Friday. That's friction dressed up as guidance. It demolishes the wall but keeps the wall's premise.

Variant 2 is also bolder in the way that matters for an aggressive dogfood round: it reframes the inbox as the topic-free deadline store it's already half-built to be, the Deadlines lens + "these are tracked — file them anytime" copy directly kills the zero-topic dead end, and it's still self-contained over setInbox + existing fields with no schema change. It's a heavier touch on deadlineEntries and InboxView render, but it's the touch that makes "Quick-add → dated card → visible on home with zero topics" actually true. That's the goal, verbatim.

As Sam (power user) I want dump-and-go dated capture that's instantly visible on home with no decision at capture time, and Variant 1 nails that with the smallest, safest surface. I verified the real dead end: deadlineEntries (src/components/widgets/util.ts:11-29) iterates ONLY ctx.topics, so any inbox-captured deadline is genuinely invisible on UpcomingDeadlines/Today, and InboxView.tsx:211-219 is a literal "create a topic in Settings → Topics" wall when dests.length===0. Variant 1 makes captureDeadline -> addTopicItem(ensureDefaultTopic()) land in a REAL topic, so it flows through the EXISTING topic loop in deadlineEntries with ZERO changes to the render util and shows up everywhere for free (UpcomingDeadlines, Today, mini-calendar). ensureDefaultTopic reuses worldToTopic/seedColdStart machinery and addTopicItem already exists (Dashboard.tsx:698) and already accepts (topicId, text, deadline), so the wiring is over existing setters with the lowest TypeScript-green risk. Crucially the bucket is renameable later (it behaves like any user topic), so I'm never locked into "Unfiled" and can promote it to a real topic keeping all dated items.\n\nI rejected Variant 2 despite it being the most elegant reframe: it's the heaviest and riskiest where it counts. deadlineEntries returns {section: SectionId} consumed by setActiveSection(e.section), and InboxItem has NO stage/completedAt/done model, so surfacing inbox deadlines means inventing a synthetic section id, dedup, and a SECOND deadline-done lifecycle living beside the topic-item one. That dual source of truth is the most likely to create subtle bugs and the most TS churn. Variant 3 has the best long-term triage affordance (inline +New topic in ChoicePicker, which I'd happily use), but as the PRIMARY kill it reintroduces a "Where does this go?" sheet at my very first dated capture, which is exactly the friction I'm trying to avoid as a power user, and it's the largest surface (QuickAddModal + InboxView + new sheet + new onCreateTopic callback). Variant 1 directly removes the structural cause (every dated capture gets a guaranteed home) with the least that can regress, which is the right call for an aggressive-but-shippable round.

changes.title: Combined cold-start screen: identity chip + real timetable on one screen (rip out fake seeded tasks)
changes.approach: Rebuild WelcomeColdStart.tsx as the chosen 'one-screen combined' flow: keep the COLD_START_OPTIONS chips at the top, but the moment a chip is selected reveal AddFeedForm INLINE on the same screen (selected = local useState, not an immediate onChoose). AddFeedForm already takes onAdd(feed), does the webcal->https rewrite (normalizeFeedUrl, line 25) and validates via fetchFeed (line 79), so embed it as-is with a one-line 'where do I find this?' helper. Change WelcomeColdStart prop to onChoose(option, feed?: CalendarFeed) and add an 'I'll add it later' affordance so the timetable is encouraged-but-skippable. In Dashboard.tsx chooseColdStart (line 354) accept the optional feed and call setCalendarFeeds(prev=>[...prev,feed]) when present (setCalendarFeeds exists at line 81; refreshFeeds at 736 auto-fetches via the effect at 755, so her real classes flow feedEvents->todayEvents->Today/calendar with no extra wiring). Drop the 'example tasks' copy. Pass a neutral dark theme + appearance.colorBank ?? [] into the embedded form.
changes.risk: Medium: AddFeedForm renders on a dark pre-theme overlay and needs a Theme(t)+colorBank, so the embed must pass a neutral/dark t and fallback bank or it renders unstyled (mitigated: AddFeedForm falls back to DEFAULT_FEED_COLORS). A failed/blank fetch must not block finishing onboarding (inline error keeps user on-screen; 'add later' still completes). No data-model or backend change.

changes.title: Stop seeding fake demo tasks; seed only the real topic + layout (keep predictTopic warm)
changes.approach: Remove the `samples` arrays (coldStart.ts:53-57,68-71,83-87,98-102) and the sample-seeding block in seedColdStart (lines 148-158 building TopicItem[] from option.samples). The returned topic must still be a real colour-coded topic with description+keywords set (145-147) so predictTopic stays non-inert (used in addToInbox Dashboard.tsx:713 and InboxRow InboxView.tsx:30) and still apply the starter home layout (starterHome line 160). Drop the unused ColdStartOption.samples field, localMidnightInDays (108-112) and DAY_MS if unused. Net: a new Uni account lands on a real topic + student layout populated by her imported timetable (change #1), not three fake tasks to delete.
changes.risk: Low: pure deletion of demo data plus dead-helper cleanup; topic/layout seeding and worldToTopic untouched. Only caller is chooseColdStart. TS stays green once the samples field and references are removed together.

changes.title: Source-level merge: deadlineEntries() ingests deadline-like calendar events so imported exams reach Deadlines/Today/MiniCalendar
changes.approach: Chosen variant = source-level merge. (1) context.ts: add optional feedEvents?: CalendarEvent[] to WidgetCtx (today only todayEvents at line 31, filtered to today, useless for the next-7-days stream). (2) Dashboard.tsx: thread the already-memoized feedEvents (line 766) into BOTH home-widget ctx objects (home grid ctx 1445-1453 and topic-page ctx 1567-1575). (3) util.ts deadlineEntries(): after the topic-item loop (14-26), iterate ctx.feedEvents and emit a DeadlineEntry for each deadline-like event = source==='deadline' OR all-day-with-keyword OR title matches /\b(due|deadline|exam|submission|coursework|assignment|hand[- ]?in)\b/i; map to synthetic ListItem {id:`feed:${e.id}`,text:e.title,status:'todo',completedAt:null,deadline:dayStart(e.start),dueMin: e.allDay?null:minutesFromMidnight}, section:'calendar', accent:e.color. Skip source==='deadline' reverse-bridged events to avoid double-counting topics. All three consumers (TodayWidget util-driven priorities line 71, UpcomingDeadlinesWidget line 77, MiniCalendarWidget line 20) fixed for free. CalendarEvent already carries allDay+source (types.ts:300/302), no model change.
changes.risk: Medium: deadline-likeness is heuristic — an all-day weekly lecture could wrongly surface; mitigate by requiring the keyword match for non-point-in-time all-day events and namespacing the synthetic id to avoid key/dedupe collisions with numeric topic ids. Guard ctx.feedEvents undefined. Pure derivation, no backend, TS-safe.

changes.title: Topic-free deadline capture: ensure-bucket helper so a zero-topic Quick-add lands a dated deadline somewhere real
changes.approach: Realize the chosen 'inbox-with-dates surfaced everywhere' intent via an ensure-bucket path needing zero pre-existing topics. In Dashboard.tsx add ensureBucketTopic() returning an existing 'Uni'/'Unfiled' topic id or, if none, creating one via worldToTopic (the way seedColdStart does, with empty items) and appending through setTopics. Add ctx method captureDeadline(text,deadline) to context.ts that parses via extractDeadline (taskParser.ts:300) when deadline is null so 'stats essay due friday 5pm' becomes dated, resolves/creates the bucket, then calls existing addTopicItem(bucketId,text,resolvedDeadline) (Dashboard.tsx:698). Wire QuickAddWidget: when a deadline is set (existing DatePicker line 49) OR text parses to a date, route through ctx.captureDeadline instead of addToInbox so it becomes a real visible deadline (and appears in the priority-#3 widgets via change #3). A plain dateless capture still goes to addToInbox (Quicks).
changes.risk: Medium: introduces auto-topic creation — guard against duplicate buckets (prefer existing same-name topic) and empty submits. extractDeadline is already battle-tested. Keep captureDeadline optional on WidgetCtx so existing ctx sites still typecheck. No schema/sync change; rides setTopics+addTopicItem.

changes.title: Fix the zero-topic Inbox wall: inline 'new topic from this Quick' instead of a dead 'go to Settings' message
changes.approach: Replace the dead-end banner at InboxView.tsx:211-219 ('no topics yet — create a topic in Settings → Topics') with an actionable inline control. Add onCreateTopic(name) prop wired in Dashboard.tsx at the InboxView usage (1536-1557) that creates a bucket via the change-#4 ensureBucketTopic/worldToTopic helper and returns its id. When dests.length===0 && inbox.length>0, render an inline 'Create a topic to file these →' input/button, plus a per-row 'new topic from this' affordance reusing the row text as the topic name, then assign immediately via the existing onAssign(text,topicId,deadline) prop (Dashboard.tsx:1541). Removes the structural stall and pairs with #4 so both capture surfaces are honest about a zero-topic state.
changes.risk: Low: additive UI plus one new prop; existing onAssign/predictTopic flow (InboxView.tsx:30,103-110) untouched. Reuses the change-#4 ensure-bucket helper so topic-creation lives in one place. Keep onCreateTopic optional to avoid breaking the contract before Dashboard is updated.

plan.summary:
Grounded change list for dogfood loop v2 round 1, anchored to the three unanimous board priorities and the user-chosen experiment variants. All paths verified by reading the real source; the agenda's line numbers came from an older layout but the files map cleanly: src/lib/coldStart.ts (samples 53-57), src/components/widgets/util.ts (deadlineEntries 11-29, topic-only), src/components/widgets/context.ts (exposes only todayEvents, not feedEvents), src/components/Dashboard.tsx (feedEvents memo 766, addTopicItem 698 requires topicId, addToInbox 712, ctx built 1445/1567), src/components/sections/InboxView.tsx (no-topics wall 217), and the three consumer widgets. The priorities chain: #1 imports the real timetable at first run, #2 makes Deadlines/Today/MiniCalendar actually see those imported exams, #3 lets a zero-topic account capture a dated deadline that lands somewhere real. #1 is split into the combined-screen UI plus the seeding teardown, with two supporting changes (ensure-bucket helper + InboxView wall fix) so the chosen variants are fully realized. No auth, payments, OAuth secrets, Supabase schema, CI, or release config is touched; every change is self-contained React/TS over existing setters and stays TypeScript-green.</parameter>
</invoke>
